articles.title: Segmentation-Guided Preprocessing Improves Deep Learning Diagnostic Accuracy and Confidence of Ameloblastoma and Odontogenic Keratocyst in Cone Beam CT Images — A Preliminary Study
articles.authors: Not fully disclosed (Peking University Hospital of Stomatology)
articles.keywords: medical image preprocessing; segmentation; explainable AI; cone-beam CT; ameloblastoma; odontogenic keratocyst
articles.abstract: Objectives: The differential diagnosis of ameloblastoma and OKC is essential for surgical planning. DL-based methods show promise but face clinical translation challenges due to insufficient interpretability. Methods: An InceptionV3 model was evaluated on 128 pathologically confirmed CBCT scans (AME:64; OKC:64) by 5-fold cross-validation comparing four input types: original slice, bounding-box ROI, precise segmentation ROI, and moderately expanded ROI. Grad-CAM and confidence curve analysis were used for XAI. Results: Precise segmentation ROI significantly improved diagnostic accuracy and confidence at the patient level. Conclusion: Segmentation-guided preprocessing supports clinical implementation of CAD systems for ameloblastoma/OKC diagnosis.

articles.title: Differential Diagnosis of OKC and SBC on Panoramic Radiographs: Leveraging Deep Learning Algorithms
articles.authors: Park JH; Kwak EJ; et al. (Pusan National University Dental Hospital)
articles.keywords: deep learning; differentiation; diagnosis; odontogenic keratocyst; simple bone cyst; panoramic radiograph
articles.abstract: Objective: To determine if deep learning can distinguish OKC from simple bone cyst (SBC) on preoperative panoramic radiographs. Methods: Retrospective analysis of 63 OKC and 125 SBC cases (2018–2022) from Pusan National University Dental Hospital. Results: CNN-based model achieved significant discrimination between OKC and SBC despite their similar radiographic appearances, both presenting as radiolucent lesions. Conclusion: Deep learning offers a promising diagnostic tool for difficult panoramic differential diagnoses involving radiolucent jaw lesions.
